mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production growth signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and operating leverage, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings expectations reprice higher.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO are not followed by positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points triggers recessionary labor market dynamics that degrade corporate revenue forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes do not produce negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns over a 5-year rolling window (mean return >= 0).

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of declining retail sales and rising unemployment generates a non-linear consumer demand collapse that forces rapid earnings multiple compression, driving negative S&P 500 returns that are larger than the sum of each signal in isolation.
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS declines and UNRATE increases does not significantly predict negative 20-day returns (interaction coefficient t-stat < 2.0) over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, month-over-month increases in CPI inflation above its recent trend accelerate expected Fed terminal rate repricing, causing immediate negative S&P 500 5-day returns as the equity risk premium expands.
failure_condition: During FEDFUNDS > 3% regimes, month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases are not followed by negative average 5-day S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling window (mean return >= 0).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, restrictive financial conditions dominate discount rate effects, causing negative S&P 500 60-day returns as leveraged corporate cash flows contract.
failure_condition: Episodes with FEDFUNDS > 5% do not produce negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling window (mean return >= 0).

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.2 million units, concurrent declines in industrial production confirm a broad supply-demand contraction that pressures corporate earnings, generating negative S&P 500 20-day returns.
failure_condition: Periods with HOUST < 1200 and negative INDPRO changes do not produce negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth above 2% annualized signals resilient aggregate demand that supports revenue expansion and positive operating leverage, driving positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes of GDPC1 growth > 2% do not produce positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns over a 5-year rolling window (mean return <= 0).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month deceleration in CPI inflation lowers the market-implied terminal Fed funds rate, compressing the equity risk premium and generating positive S&P 500 5-day returns as discount rates fall.
failure_condition: Month-over-month CPIAUCSL decelerations are not followed by positive average 5-day S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling window (mean return <= 0).
